metadata.title: Why web search never runs your code but your own tool always does — with identical syntax
metadata.purpose: Round-trip count is set by who can execute — server-side tools need infrastructure (web index, sandbox) Anthropic runs in-turn; client-side tools are arbitrary device code only the caller can run, forcing an exit-and-return.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: raster (two-lane timeline), c2v (config vs execution path)
metadata.prerequisites: tool-calling basics, async request-response, API round-trips
metadata.exclusions: domain filtering, maxUses rate limiting, tool-result schema
metadata.visual_object: Two-lane timeline — server tool's request bubble closes on itself; client tool's request → device callback → second request
metadata.hook: Every tool call looks the same from the model's side, yet server-side tools finish inside one API turn while client-side tools must exit to the device and re-enter with a second request.

beats.narration_text: An app declares `.webSearch(maxUses:5)` in `serverTools:` and a Swift `lookupFavorites()` in `tools:`. Web search: Anthropic's servers run it, results embed — one request. `lookupFavorites`: the model emits a tool-use block, the framework calls the device function, the app sends the result in a second request. Symmetric config, asymmetric latency.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: A tool call should always round-trip through the caller's code; the web-search call did not; why?
Every tool call looks the same from the model's side, yet server-side tools finish inside one API turn while client-side tools must exit to the device and re-enter with a second request.

Round-trip count is set by who can execute — server-side tools need infrastructure (web index, sandbox) Anthropic runs in-turn; client-side tools are arbitrary device code only the caller can run, forcing an exit-and-return.
beats.visual: Two-lane timeline — server tool's request bubble closes on itself; client tool's request → device callback → second request
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: "Search 'osmosis,' then check my notes." Web search: one request, results embedded. `checkNotes()`: emit call → device runs it → second request.

Round-trip count is set by who can execute — server-side tools need infrastructure (web index, sandbox) Anthropic runs in-turn; client-side tools are arbitrary device code only the caller can run, forcing an exit-and-return.
